title: Protocol Outcomes, Layers, Standards, and Evidence · Runtime Contract: Transfer Evidence Plan
summary: A team says it built HTTP from scratch because one request works, but it cannot separate application semantics, wire syntax, transport behavior, implementation policy, or user and operator evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transfer evidence plan increment in protocol outcome, layer, authority, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Plan deterministic browser models, Go compilation, net.Pipe tests, loopback sockets, TLS, packet capture, proxy, fuzz, race, load, and production transfer gates.
Separate required conformance, optional tolerance, local policy, registered extensions, and unsupported behavior in parser decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Transfer Evidence Plan · Theory Transfer Evidence Plan
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 4390 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan deterministic browser models, Go compilation, net.Pipe tests, loopback sockets, TLS, packet capture, proxy, fuzz, race, load, and production transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Transfer Evidence Plan contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Transfer Evidence Plan · Theory Transfer Evidence Plan.

For Predict Transfer Evidence Plan · Theory Transfer Evidence Plan, keep Transfer Evidence Plan connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Build Transfer Evidence Plan evidence · Theory Transfer Evidence Plan
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-transfer-evidence-plan-14ksce" that uses case 7494 to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.
